When Christ finished his work on earth and was received into his glory in heaven, the church began to sing a new song to him and to bless his name. His apostles and evangelists showed forth his salvation among the heathen and his wonders among all people. All the earth is summoned to worship the Lord. We must worship him in the beauty of holiness, as God in Christ, reconciling the world to himself. Glorious things are said of him, both as motives to praise and as matter of praise.

We are to hope and pray for that time when Christ shall reign in righteousness over all nations. He will rule in the hearts of men by the power of truth and the Spirit of righteousness. His coming draws near; this King, this Judge stands before the door, but he is not yet come. The Lord will accept the praises of all who seek to promote the kingdom of Christ. The sea can only roar, and how the trees of the wood can show that they rejoice we do not know; but He who searches the heart knows what is the mind of the Spirit and understands the words, the broken language of the weakest. Christ will come to judge the earth, to execute just vengeance on his enemies, and to fulfill his largest promises to his people. What then are we? Would that day be welcome to us? If this is not our case, let us now begin to prepare to meet our God by seeking the pardon of our sins and the renewal of our souls to holiness.
